major: Sociology, Community Studies Emphasis, B.A. (Transfer Starting Fall 2025) *updated*

courses.title: American Social Issues: Problems and Challenges
Research Methods for the Social Sciences

notes: Between the Fall 2025 and Fall 2027 application cycles, the School of Social Sciences, Humanities and Arts will not require transfer students to complete major specific selection criteria to be admitted to this major*. Students wanting to enroll in this major will be admitted using UC Merced's general transfer requirements**. * This only applies to certain SSHA majors. ** The General Transfer Requirements are as f ol lows: Units and GPA Complete 60 semester (90 quarter) units of UC-transferable college credit Earn a minimum grade point average of 2.40 (nonresident: 2.80) in UC-transferable courses Take no more than 14 semester (21 quarter) units as Pass/Not Pass Course Requirements/Subject Requirements Complete the following seven-course pattern, earning a grade of C- or better in each course: Two transferable English composition courses One transferable mathematical concepts and quantitative reasoning course that has a published prerequisite of intermediate algebra Four transferable courses chosen from at least two of the following areas: Arts and Humanities, Behavioral and Social Sciences, Biological and Physical Sciences While major courses are not required for admissions, it is highly recommended to complete as many of the recommended major preparatory courses as possible before transferring to ensure timely progress to degree. Any major courses not completed prior to enrollment at UC Merced must be completed upon enrollment at UC Merced to ensure time to degree and graduation. Recommended, but not Required Prior to Transfer: SOC 001 At least one course from the following course ranges: SOC 011 - SOC 014 or SOC 016 - SOC 089 Complete the additional Lower Division Emphasis Track course: SOC 009 (Must be taken at UC Merced after Transfer). Complete the following courses: SOC 010 and SOC 015 (Highly recommended to be completed before transfer). Courses in the major must be taken for a letter grade and may not be taken on a pass/no pass basis unless the course is only offered on a pass/no pass basis. AP Exam Score & Course Exemptions An AP Statistics score of 5 exempts SOC 010 UC Merced Advance Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) credit policies are detailed in the link below: A dvance Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examinations For more questions about admissions, please email: transfer@ucmerced.edu .
